steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Reading comprehension — hypothetical consequence. The passage says Hale began using sewing machines "As he gained more customers," to "produce bags more efficiently,". Identify what would have happened without that change.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: The machines were adopted in response to growing demand, so without them he would have struggled to keep up with that demand.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — He would have been unable to ensure that each bag included unique, handcrafted details.: backwards — the passage says he "continued to handcraft the crucial details that made each bag unique." so handcrafting did not depend on machines. ✗
  • Choice C — He would have had to individually design each bag he produced.: the text never discusses designing each bag individually; machines were about production efficiency, not design. Out of scope. ✗
  • Choice D — He would not have been able to generate as much interest in his bags.: interest came from his craftsmanship and reputation, not from machine use; the passage links machines only to output volume. ✗
That is the correct answer.
